description.en: Verona's Christmas centerpiece: a towering star-shaped loaf of butter-rich egg dough, gold throughout, with no candied fruit to distract from its vanilla scent. Sliced into eight-point stars and dusted heavily with powdered sugar for Christmas Eve.
description.de: Veronas Weihnachtsstern: ein hoher, sternförmiger Kuchen aus butterreichem Eierteig, golden bis in die Krume, ganz ohne kandierte Früchte. In Sternscheiben geschnitten und dick mit Vanille-Puderzucker bestäubt, krönt er den Heiligabend.
description.es: La estrella navideña de Verona: un pan alto en forma de estrella de ocho puntas, de masa riquísima en huevo y mantequilla, sin fruta confitada. Cortado en rodajas estrelladas y cubierto de azúcar glas con vainilla, corona la Nochebuena.
description.fr: La star de Noël véronaise : une brioche haute en étoile à huit branches, dorée dedans comme dehors, sans aucun fruit confit. Tranchée en étoiles et généreusement poudrée de sucre vanillé, elle clôt en douceur le réveillon italien.

origin.note: Created in 1894 by Veronese pastry chef Domenico Melegatti, who patented the eight-point star mold; it's now Italy's second Christmas bread after panettone.

ingredients.note: 95 g in the overnight starter, 330 g in the main dough
about 2 g in the starter, 9 g in the main dough
4 g in the starter, 100 g in the main dough
vanilla bean paste, or 2 tsp extract
powdered sugar with vanilla, for dusting after baking

keeping.note: Wrap airtight in foil or a bag; without candied fruit to hold moisture the crumb dries a little faster than panettone.

method.title: Overnight starter
method.summary: The evening before, stir water, a little sugar, yeast and flour into a firm dough. Let it double at room temperature, then refrigerate overnight to build flavor.

method.title: Temper and mix
method.summary: Let the chilled starter lose its chill, then combine it with warmed milk, yeast, flour, sugar and egg yolks. Knead into a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: Work in the butter
method.summary: With the mixer running, add softened butter a little at a time along with the honey, vanilla and lemon zest, letting each addition disappear before the next so the gluten stretches around the fat instead of tearing.

method.title: Bulk rise
method.summary: Cover and let the rich dough triple in bulk somewhere warm; the sugar and butter slow the yeast, so this takes several hours.

method.title: Pan and proof
method.summary: Butter and flour the star mold thoroughly so every point releases cleanly, drop in the dough, and let it rise until it crests just above the rim.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at a moderate temperature, lowering the heat partway through, until deep gold and a skewer through the center comes out clean.

method.title: Cool and dust
method.summary: Cool briefly in the mold, then turn the loaf out fully before slicing it into star-shaped discs and burying them in vanilla-scented powdered sugar just before serving.

source.adaptation: Paraphrased the starter-then-main-dough method; cross-checked the salt level and gradual butter incorporation against a second traditional recipe.
